docs.title: Generative AI Modeling for Extreme Events
docs.text:
GAMEX (Generative AI Modeling for Extreme Events: Statistical Foundations) is a Una Europa seed-funded initiative (2026) building an international research network at the intersection of Generative Artificial Intelligence and Extreme Value Theory (EVT).

The project brings together researchers in statistics, machine learning, and data science to advance reliable and interpretable methods for modeling rare, high-impact events, with applications ranging from climate risk to finance and environmental sciences.

docs.title: Why GAMEX
docs.text:
Extreme events such as floods, heatwaves, and financial crises are increasingly central to scientific analysis and decision-making. While modern generative models scale efficiently to complex, high-dimensional data, they often fail to reproduce tail behaviour and extreme dependence structures.

GAMEX addresses this gap by fostering collaboration between experts in EVT and Generative AI, with the goal of developing models that are both scalable and theoretically grounded.

docs.title: Key activities in 2026
docs.text:
• Scientific Workshop University of Bologna, 11–12 June 2026

  • Summer School on Generative AI for Extremes University of Edinburgh, 8–11 September 2026

  • Online Seminar Series Regular talks by GAMEX partners and invited external speakers

  • Short-term Research Visits Mobility opportunities for PhD students and early-career researchers (ECRs)

docs.title: A platform for long-term collaboration
docs.text: GAMEX is conceived as a platform, not a stand-alone project. Beyond its 2026 activities, the network aims to support future collaborative initiatives and to expand through additional academic and industrial partnerships.

docs.title: Get involved
docs.text:
• Interested in giving a talk? → see Seminar Series
  • Interested in a research visit? → see Mobility
  • Interested in joining the network? → see Contact

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
For general inquiries, seminars, mobility opportunities, or partnership proposals, please contact:

  • Luca Trapin — University of Bologna Principal Investigator, GAMEX

  • Miguel de Carvalho — University of Edinburgh Summer School & Seminar Series

docs.title: Acknowledgements
docs.text: GAMEX is funded by Una Europa under the Seed Funding initiative (2026).

docs.title: GAMEX Scientific Workshop — Bologna
docs.text:
Dates: 11–12 June 2026 Location: University of Bologna

The workshop will bring together GAMEX partners and invited external speakers to discuss recent advances at the intersection of Generative AI and Extreme Value Theory.

Format - Research talks - Thematic discussions - Strategic session on future collaborations and funding

Programme and registration details will be announced in due course.

docs.title: GAMEX Summer School — Edinburgh
docs.text:
Dates: 8–11 September 2026 Location: University of Edinburgh

An intensive training event aimed at PhD students and early-career researchers.

Topics - Generative models for extremes - EVT foundations for high-dimensional data - Hands-on tutorials and case studies

An open call for participants will be published in 2026.

docs.title: Mobility (PhD / ECR Research Visits)
docs.text: GAMEX supports short-term research visits among partner institutions to foster collaboration, skills transfer, and joint research.

docs.title: Who can apply
docs.text:
• PhD students
  • Early-career researchers affiliated with GAMEX partner institutions

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: Visits are intended to: - develop joint research projects - exchange methodological expertise - strengthen long-term collaboration within the network

docs.title: Expression of interest
docs.text: Interested candidates should contact the Mobility coordinator with: - Host institution and academic contact - Tentative dates and duration - Brief research plan (1–2 paragraphs)

docs.title: External collaborators
docs.text: Additional academic and industrial collaborators will be listed here as the network expands. people.md

docs.title: Project
docs.text: GAMEX (Generative AI Modeling for Extreme Events: Statistical Foundations) is a Una Europa seed-funded initiative (Jan–Dec 2026) building an international research community at the interface of Generative AI and Extreme Value Theory (EVT).

docs.title: Motivation
docs.text: Extreme events (e.g., floods, heatwaves, financial tail risk) are increasingly relevant for decision-making. While modern generative models scale to high-dimensional data, they often struggle to reproduce tail behaviour reliably. GAMEX connects the scalability of Generative AI with the rigor and interpretability of EVT to foster robust modeling of rare, high-impact events.

docs.title: Objectives
docs.text:
• Scientific advancement: strengthen collaboration on methods that integrate Generative AI and EVT for modeling extremes.
  • Community building and training: support early-career researchers through seminars, events, and short-term visits.
  • Strategic development: expand the network with additional academic and industrial partners and lay foundations for future funding initiatives (e.g., MSCA Staff Exchange, Doctoral Networks).

docs.title: 2026 activities (at a glance)
docs.text:
• Online seminar series (throughout 2026)
  • Short-term research visits (PhD/ECR mobility)
  • Scientific Workshop — Bologna, 11–12 June 2026
  • Summer School — Edinburgh, 8–11 September 2026

docs.title: Online Seminar Series
docs.text: GAMEX hosts an online seminar series throughout 2026 to stimulate scientific exchange within the network and beyond.

docs.title: Format
docs.text:
• Online talks (approx. 60 minutes)
  • Speakers from GAMEX partners and external institutions
  • Open to PhD students and early-career researchers

docs.title: Propose a speaker
docs.text:
To propose a speaker (internal or external), please contact the seminar coordinators with: - Name and affiliation - Tentative title and short abstract - Preferred time window

Contact details are available on the Contact page.
